data.title: Bitcoin ETF trimmed as Harvard adds $86.8M Ethereum ETF
data.description:
Harvard Management Company trimmed its Bitcoin ETF by ~21% and initiated an $86. 8M Ethereum ETF stake; analysts cite rebalancing and measured positioning.
source: https://coincu.com/news/bitcoin-etf-trimmed-as-harvard-adds-86-8m-ethereum-etf/

data.title: Nvidia Expands AI Initiatives in India with New Partnerships
data.description:
Nvidia is intensifying its focus on India through a range of local partnerships and infrastructure investments aimed at boosting the country's artificial intelligence capabilities. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Nvidia's strategic move to support India's growing tech landscape.

The company is collaborating with Indian firms to enhance AI infrastructure, which is expected to play a crucial role in the nation's technological advancement. These partnerships are part of Nvidia's broader strategy to tap into India's burgeoning market and contribute to its digital transformation.

By investing in local initiatives, Nvidia aims to foster innovation and support the development of AI technologies in India. This move aligns with the country's ambitions to become a global leader in AI and digital solutions.

Nvidia's commitment to India reflects the increasing importance of the region in the global tech industry, as companies seek to leverage local talent and resources to drive growth and innovation.

data.title: Indian Central Bank's Proposal to Tighten Control on Offshore Rupee Transactions
data.description: The Reserve Bank of India's recent proposal mandates banks to report their offshore rupee transactions, a move analysts believe will strengthen the central bank's control over the currency market. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this initiative is part of a broader strategy to monitor and regulate currency flows more effectively. Analysts suggest that this measure could lead to increased transparency and oversight in the foreign exchange market, potentially impacting the rupee's volatility and liquidity. The proposal reflects the central bank's ongoing efforts to stabilize the currency and ensure a more controlled economic environment.

data.title: Peter Thiel Sells Entire Stake in Ethereum Treasury Firm ETHZilla
data.description: Billionaire investor Peter Thiel has completely divested his holdings in the Ethereum treasury firm ETHZilla. Crypto KOL Ted posted on X. The reasons behind Thiel's decision to exit ETHZilla remain unclear, but there is speculation that he might be shifting his focus to other ventures, such as Bitmine. Thiel's move has sparked discussions within the cryptocurrency community about potential strategic shifts in his investment portfolio.

data.title: STOCKS | Palantir's Stock Rises Amid Headquarters Relocation and Strong Earnings
data.description:
Palantir Technologies Inc. saw its stock increase by 1.2% following the announcement of its headquarters move from Colorado to Miami. According to NS3.AI, this decision surprised both local officials and investors. The company reported strong fourth-quarter earnings, showcasing positive revenue growth and surpassing earnings expectations. This has boosted short-term optimism, despite ongoing concerns about its high price-to-earnings ratio and recent insider selling.

Market analysts remain divided on Palantir's future, issuing conflicting price targets that contribute to uncertainty regarding the stock's medium- to long-term outlook.

data.title: Expert Warns of Potential U.S. Stock Market Bubble Burst
data.description: Mark Spitznagel, founder of Universa Investments, predicts that the U.S. stock market will continue its upward trajectory in the short term, driven by a favorable economic climate characterized by easing inflation and interest rates. According to NS3.AI, Spitznagel cautions that this growth represents the final phase of what he describes as the largest bubble in history, which is poised to burst eventually. He suggests that the S&P 500 could climb to 8,000 points before experiencing a significant downturn, particularly if the Federal Reserve keeps interest rates unchanged for an extended period.

data.title: The founder of a well-known "Black Swan Fund" warns: The S&P 500 may plummet after reaching 8,000 points.
data.description: Mark Spitznagel, founder and chief investment officer of the “Black Swan Fund” Universa Investments, says the multi-year rally in U.S. stocks is far from over—at least for now. In a recent letter to investors, Spitznagel wrote that the market will continue in the “Goldilocks zone—declining inflation and interest rates, a slowing but not excessive economic slowdown, and a shift to market frenzy—with the stock market continuing to climb and ending in a surge.” However, he added that “the biggest bubble in human history” is now in its final stages. Spitznagel’s hedge fund, which has been operating for nearly two decades, focuses on tail risk hedging, protecting investors’ portfolios from the next major crash. He says that as long as the economy remains resilient, the stock market will continue to rise—a view he has maintained since late 2022. In an interview, he stated that market frenzy could push the S&P 500 to 8,000 points or even higher—followed by a sharp reversal. A worrying aspect is that if the Federal Reserve maintains current interest rates for an extended period, businesses will begin to struggle to raise capital. Spitznagel stated that while the economy appears resilient, monetary policy exhibits a lag effect, and the Federal Reserve's excessive focus on lagging indicators like inflation has become outdated. "The Fed is currently holding back, and as the economy gradually deteriorates, the market will anticipate more easing measures," he said. In this context, the stock market will climb on expectations of further rate cuts, followed by a rapid decline when the economy slows. "At some point, the Fed will be powerless to reverse the situation, exactly as happened in 2007 and 2008."

data.title: Investor Huang Licheng Faces Significant Losses Amid High-Leverage Positions
data.description:
Investor Huang Licheng is experiencing substantial financial losses due to high-leverage trading positions. According to ChainCatcher, despite these setbacks, Huang continues to increase leverage and open new positions. His current unrealized losses exceed $27.8 million.

Huang's current long positions include $13.08 million in ETH with 25x leverage, $1.69 million in BTC with 40x leverage, $1.29 million in HYPE with 10x leverage, and $38,000 in VVV with 3x leverage.

data.title: Peter Thiel's Founders Fund Completes Exit from ETHZilla
data.description: Billionaire tech investor Peter Thiel and his Founders Fund have completely divested from ETHZilla, as indicated by a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strategic move by Thiel's investment firm. The decision marks a significant shift in the fund's investment strategy, reflecting broader trends in the tech investment landscape. The exit from ETHZilla underscores the dynamic nature of venture capital investments and the evolving priorities of major investors like Thiel.

data.title: Sanae Takaichi Secures Majority in Japan's Prime Ministerial Election
data.description: Sanae Takaichi, leader of Japan's Liberal Democratic Party, has won the majority vote in the first round of the Prime Ministerial election held in the House of Representatives. According to Jin10, this victory ensures her position as Japan's Prime Minister, as the House of Representatives' election results take precedence under the Japanese Constitution. The next step involves the Prime Ministerial election in the House of Councillors.

data.title: Sanae Takaichi is basically confirmed to be Japan's new prime minister.
data.description: On February 18th local time, in the first round of voting in the House of Representatives' prime ministerial nomination election in Japan, Liberal Democratic Party President Sanae Takaichi won with a majority of votes, securing victory. The House of Councillors election will follow. Because the Japanese Constitution stipulates that the House of Representatives election results have "priority," it is certain that Sanae Takaichi will be elected Prime Minister of Japan. (CCTV)

data.title: Moonwell incurred approximately $1.78 million in bad debts due to oracle misconfiguration.
data.description: DeFi lending protocol Moonwell suffered approximately $1.78 million in bad debt due to an oracle misconfiguration. After enabling Chainlink's OEV wrapper contract via governance proposal MIP-X43 on February 15th, Moonwell experienced a serious misconfiguration of its cbETH price oracle, which at one point valued the currency at only $1. On-chain trading bots and liquidators acted swiftly, seizing cbETH collateral by repaying approximately $1 of debt. This misconfiguration resulted in the liquidation of 1096.317 cbETH, wiping out the vast majority of collateral for many borrowers and leaving the protocol with a significant amount of bad debt. The Moonwell team immediately reduced the cbETH borrowing and supply caps to 0.01 after discovering the issue to limit further losses, but the official team has declined to comment further on the source of the error or details of the vulnerability.
